statement.p: Carly and Carly are working together to paint a room. If Carly paints the room alone, it would take her 16 hours to complete the job. If Carly paints the room alone, it would take her 5 hours to complete the job. Answer the following question:
If they work together, it would take them hours to complete the job. Use a decimal in your answer if needed.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a person 5 hours to complete a job. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{5} of the job.
Similarly, if it takes a person x hours to complete a job, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{x} of the job.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Its given that, if Carly paints the room alone, it would take her 16 hours to complete the job. Each hour, Carly does \frac{1}{16} of the whole job.
Similarly, if Carly paints the room alone, it would take her 5 hours to complete the job. Each hour, Carly does \frac{1}{5} of the whole job.
Assume that, if they work together, it would take them x hours to complete the job. Thus, each hour, they can complete \frac{1}{x} of the whole job.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {16\cdot 5x}.
By the solution we got, if they work together, it would take them {3.80952} hours to complete the job.
